session1.context: James is putting a special restorative stain on the entire surface of the wooden chest. One can of stain covers about 35 square feet.

problem_stems.guided: James is putting a special restorative stain on the entire surface of the wooden chest. One can of stain covers about 35 square feet. How many cans of stain will James need?
problem_stems.collaborative: What is the area of each face?

vocabulary.definition: The surface area of a three-dimensional figure is the sum of the areas of the faces.
A tool used to deconstruct a three-dimensional figure into its flat faces.
A three-dimensional figure with 6 rectangular faces with 3 pairs of congruent faces.
A flat surface of a three-dimensional figure.
Having the same size and shape.

misconceptions: Students may forget to include all 6 faces when calculating surface area.
Students may confuse surface area with volume.
Students may not recognize that opposite faces of a rectangular prism are congruent, leading to redundant calculations.

session2.context: James is putting a special restorative stain on the entire surface of the wooden chest. One can of stain covers about 35 square feet.

problem_stems.guided: How many cans of stain will James need to cover the wooden chest with a surface area of 69 square feet if one can covers about 35 square feet?
problem_stems.collaborative: How can you find the surface area of the rectangular prism?

vocabulary.definition: The surface area of a three-dimensional figure is the sum of the areas of the faces.
A tool used to deconstruct a three-dimensional figure into its flat faces.
A three-dimensional figure with 6 rectangular faces with 3 pairs of congruent faces.
A flat surface of a three-dimensional figure.
Having the same size and shape.

misconceptions: Students may round down the number of cans needed instead of rounding up, resulting in an insufficient quantity.
Students may confuse surface area with volume when determining how much stain is needed.
Students may incorrectly apply the division (69 ÷ 35) and not interpret the remainder correctly in context.
